open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped InnerProductSpace

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
  [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]
variable {S : Type*} [MeasurableSpace S]
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]

-- ============================================================================
-- SubgradientSetup: NO gradF field (non-smooth objective)
-- ============================================================================
/-- Complete setup for stochastic subgradient method.
Critical distinction: contains NO `gradF` field (non-smooth objective has no gradient).
Includes oracle measurability `hgL` as structure field (Convention §2). -/
structure SubgradientSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  w₀ : E
  η : ℕ → ℝ
  gradL : E → S → E
  ξ : ℕ → Ω → S
  P : Measure Ω
  hP : IsProbabilityMeasure P
  hξ_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (ξ t)
  hξ_indep : iIndepFun (β := fun _ => S) ξ P
  hξ_ident : ∀ t, IdentDistrib (ξ t) (ξ 0) P P
  hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry gradL)

/-- Self-contained recursive process definition for subgradient method (Archetype B). -/
noncomputable def SubgradientSetup.process (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω) : ℕ → Ω → E
  | 0 => fun _ => setup.w₀
  | t + 1 => fun ω =>
      let w_t := process setup t ω
      w_t - setup.η t • setup.gradL w_t (setup.ξ t ω)

namespace SubgradientSetup

variable (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω)

@[simp]
theorem process_zero : process setup 0 = fun _ => setup.w₀ := by
  rfl

@[simp]
theorem process_succ (t : ℕ) :
    process setup (t + 1) =
      fun ω => process setup t ω - setup.η t • setup.gradL (process setup t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) := by
  rfl

end SubgradientSetup

-- ============================================================================
-- Convergence theorem (PRIMITIVE FORM — NO abstract subdifferential symbols)
-- ============================================================================
/-- Convex convergence for stochastic subgradient method (primitive inequality form).
Archetype B: novel proof structure bypassing Layer 1 meta-theorems.
Uses primitive subgradient condition: ∀ w s y, f y ≥ f w + ⟪gradL w s, y - w⟫_ℝ
Derives variance bound internally from uniform oracle bound (Pattern I).
Used in: main result for non-smooth convex stochastic optimization (this file) -/
theorem subgradient_convergence_convex
    (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω) (f : E → ℝ) {G η : ℝ} (wStar : E)
    (hsubgrad : ∀ w s y, f y ≥ f w + ⟪setup.gradL w s, y - w⟫_ℝ)
    (hbounded : ∀ w s, ‖setup.gradL w s‖ ≤ G)
    (hη_pos : 0 < η) (hη : ∀ t, setup.η t = η)
    (T : ℕ) (hT : 0 < T)
    (h_int_f : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω => f (setup.process t ω)) setup.P)
    (h_int_norm_sq : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.P)
    (h_int_sq : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) setup.P) :
    (1 / (T : ℝ)) * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T,
        (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤
      ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 / (2 * η * T) + η * G ^ 2 / 2 := by
  -- Use the recursive definition of the process
  have h_succ : ∀ t, setup.process (t + 1) = fun ω => setup.process t ω - setup.η t • setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) := by
    intro t
    rw [process_succ]
    rfl

  -- We'll prove the bound by expanding the norm squared of the distance to the optimum
  -- and using the subgradient property to telescope the sum
  have key_bound : ∀ t, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P ≤
                  ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - 
                  2 * η * ∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2 := by
    intro t
    -- Apply norm expansion using norm_sq_sgd_step
    have h_expansion : ∀ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 = 
                       ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 - 
                       2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + 
                       (setup.η t) ^ 2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2 := by
      intro ω
      rw [h_succ, norm_sq_sgd_step]

    -- Integrate both sides
    have h_integrated : ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P =
                        ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - 
                        2 * setup.η t * ∫ ω, ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ ∂setup.P + 
                        ∫ ω, (setup.η t) ^ 2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P := by
      rw [h_expansion]
      -- Apply integral operations
      have h_int1 : Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.P := h_int_norm_sq t
      -- Show integrability of the inner product term
      have h_int2 : Integrable (fun ω => ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ) setup.P := by
        -- From the hypothesis h_int_norm_sq t, we have Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.P
        -- From the hypothesis h_int_sq t, we have Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) setup.P
        -- Using Cauchy-Schwarz inequality: |⟪u,v⟫| ≤ ‖u‖·‖v‖ ≤ (‖u‖^2 + ‖v‖^2)/2
        have h_bound : ∀ ω, |⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ| ≤
                             (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 + ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) / 2 := by
          intro ω
          calc |⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ|
              ≤ ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ := abs_real_inner_le_norm _ _
            _ ≤ (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 + ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) / 2 := by
                -- Prove that for real numbers a, b: a*b <= (a^2 + b^2)/2
                -- This follows from 0 <= (a-b)^2 = a^2 - 2ab + b^2, so 2ab <= a^2 + b^2
                have h_ineq : (2 : ℝ) * ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ≤
                            ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 + ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2 := by
                  have h_sq : 0 ≤ (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ - ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖) ^ 2 :=
                    pow_two_nonneg (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ - ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖)
                  rw [pow_two, sub_mul_self] at h_sq
                  linarith
                field_simp at h_ineq
                linarith
        have h_sum_integrable : Integrable (fun ω => (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 + ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) / 2) setup.P :=
          ((h_int_norm_sq t).add (h_int_sq t)).smul (2⁻¹ : ℝ)
        apply Integrable.mono' h_sum_integrable
        intro ω
        exact h_bound ω
      have h_int3 : Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) setup.P := h_int_sq t
      rw [integral_add h_int1 (integral_smul (-2 * setup.η t) h_int2)]
      rw [integral_smul (setup.η t ^ 2) h_int3]
      ring

    -- Use the subgradient inequality: f y ≥ f w + ⟪gradL w s, y - w⟫
    -- With y = wStar and w = setup.process t ω, we get:
    -- f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫
    have h_subgrad_ineq : ∀ ω, f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫_ℝ := by
      intro ω
      specialize hsubgrad (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) wStar
      linarith

    -- Rearrange to get: ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar
    have h_rearranged : ∀ ω, ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar := by
      intro ω
      have h := h_subgrad_ineq ω
      rw [inner_neg_right, neg_sub] at h
      linarith

    -- Apply this to the integral term
    have h_integral_ineq : ∫ ω, ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ ∂setup.P ≥
                           ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P := by
      apply integral_mono
      . assumption -- integrable on the right
      . apply h_int_f -- integrability of f(process(t,ω))
      . intro ω
        rw [inner_comm]
        exact h_rearranged ω

    -- Use the bounded gradient condition to bound the last term
    have h_bounded_term : ∫ ω, (setup.η t) ^ 2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P ≤ η ^ 2 * G ^ 2 := by
      rw [← integral_const_mul, ← hη t]
      apply integral_mono_const_of_ae_le
      intro ω
      rw [sq_le_sq]
      apply pow_mono
      . exact hbounded (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)
      . norm_num

    -- Combine all bounds
    rw [h_integrated]
    linarith [h_integral_ineq, h_bounded_term]

  -- Now we can telescope the sum
  have h_telescope : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤
                     (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) / (2 * η) + T * η * G ^ 2 / 2 := by
    -- Sum up the key bound from t=0 to t=T-1
    have h_sum : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (2 * η * (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar)) ≤
                 ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - 
                                       ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) + T * η ^ 2 * G ^ 2 := by
      apply Finset.sum_le_sum
      intro t ht
      -- Note: key_bound works for t < T, but we need to ensure t < T for our loop
      have h : ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P ≤
               ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - 
               2 * η * ∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2 := by
        apply key_bound
      -- Rearrange to get the desired inequality
      linarith

    -- The middle sum telescopes
    have h_telescope_sum : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - 
                                                  ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) =
                           ∫ ω, ‖setup.process 0 ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P := by
      rw [Finset.sum_sub_distrib]
      -- Telescoping property
      rw [Finset.sum_range_sub_sum_succ]
      . intro t ht
        apply h_int_norm_sq
      . intro t ht
        apply h_int_norm_sq

    -- Simplify using process_zero
    rw [process_zero, h_telescope_sum]
    rw [Pi.zero_apply]
    rw [← sub_eq_add_neg]
    rw [mul_comm (2 * η)]
    -- Multiply both sides by 1/(2*η)
    have h_div : (2 * η) > 0 := by linarith [hη_pos]
    have h_div_ne : (2 * η) ≠ 0 := by linarith [hη_pos]
    field_simp [h_div_ne] at h_sum
    rw [Finset.sum_const, nsmul_eq_mul] at h_sum
    rw [mul_comm (2 * η)] at h_sum
    rw [mul_assoc, ← mul_sub] at h_sum
    rw [mul_comm (T : ℝ)] at h_sum
    field_simp [mul_comm, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm] at h_sum
    -- Divide by 2*η
    rw [div_eq_mul_one_div, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc, mul_one_div_cancel_left (by linarith [hη_pos])] at h_sum
    exact h_sum

  -- Final rearrangement
  have h_final : (1 / (T : ℝ)) * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤
                 (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) / (2 * η * T) + η * G ^ 2 / 2 := by
    rw [div_eq_mul_inv, mul_assoc]
    have h_div : (2 * η) * (T : ℝ) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [mul_ne_zero_iff]
      constructor
      . linarith [hη_pos]
      . exact Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr (NeZero.ne T)
    field_simp [h_div]
    rw [mul_comm, div_mul_eq_mul_div]
    linarith [h_telescope]

  -- Since the squared norm is non-negative, we can drop the negative integral term
  apply le_trans h_final
  apply add_le_add_right
  apply div_le_div_of_pos
  . apply sub_le_self
    apply integral_nonneg
    intro ω
    apply pow_two_nonneg
  . apply mul_pos
    apply mul_pos
    . exact hη_pos
    . exact cast_pos.mpr hT

          calc |⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ|
              ≤ ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ := abs_real_inner_le_norm _ _
            _ ≤ (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 + ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) / 2 := by
                -- Prove that for real numbers a, b: a*b <= (a^2 + b^2)/2
                -- This follows from 0 <= (a-b)^2 = a^2 - 2ab + b^2, so 2ab <= a^2 + b^2

                  have h_sq : 0 ≤ (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ - ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖) ^ 2 :=
                    pow_two_nonneg (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ - ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖)
                  rw [pow_two, sub_mul_self] at h_sq
                  linarith
                field_simp at h_ineq
                linarith
        have h_sum_integrable : Integrable (fun ω => (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 + ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) / 2) setup.P :=
          ((h_int_norm_sq t).add (h_int_sq t)).smul (2⁻¹ : ℝ)
        apply Integrable.mono' h_sum_integrable
        intro ω
        exact h_bound ω
      have h_int3 : Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) setup.P := h_int_sq t
      rw [integral_add h_int1 (integral_smul (-2 * setup.η t) h_int2)]
      rw [integral_smul (setup.η t ^ 2) h_int3]
      ring

    -- Use the subgradient inequality: f y ≥ f w + ⟪gradL w s, y - w⟫
    -- With y = wStar and w = setup.process t ω, we get:
    -- f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫
    have h_subgrad_ineq : ∀ ω, f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫_ℝ := by
      intro ω
      specialize hsubgrad (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) wStar

    -- Rearrange to get: ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar
    have h_rearranged : ∀ ω, ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar := by
      intro ω
      have h := h_subgrad_ineq ω
      rw [inner_neg_right, neg_sub] at h
      linarith

    have h_integral_ineq : ∫ ω, ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ ∂setup.P ≥
                           ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P := by
      -- Need to show integrability of (fun ω => f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar)
      have h_int_diff : Integrable (fun ω => f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar) setup.P :=

                           ∫ ω, ‖setup.process 0 ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P := by
      rw [Finset.sum_sub_distrib]

        apply h_int_norm_sq
      . intro t ht
        apply h_int_norm_sq

    rw [process_zero, h_telescope_sum]
    rw [Pi.zero_apply]
    rw [← sub_eq_add_neg]
    rw [mul_comm (2 * η)]
    -- Multiply both sides by 1/(2*η)
    have h_div : (2 * η) > 0 := by linarith [hη_pos]

    rw [mul_assoc, ← mul_sub] at h_sum

  have h_final : (1 / (T : ℝ)) * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤
                 (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) / (2 * η * T) + η * G ^ 2 / 2 := by
    rw [div_eq_mul_inv, mul_assoc]

      rw [mul_ne_zero_iff]
      constructor
      . linarith [hη_pos]
      . exact Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr (NeZero.ne T)
    field_simp [h_div]
    rw [mul_comm, div_mul_eq_mul_div]
    linarith [h_telescope]

  apply le_trans h_final
  apply add_le_add_right
  apply div_le_div_of_pos
  . apply sub_le_self
    apply integral_nonneg

    . exact hη_pos
    . exact cast_pos.mpr hT

      rw [← integral_const_mul, ← hη t]

  have h_telescope : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤

    -- ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P -

    -- Rearranging: 2 * η * ∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P ≤
    -- ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2

         2 * η * (∫ ω, f (setup.process 0 ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) +

                                   ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2) := by

  "after": "import Main
import Lib.Glue.Algebra
import Lib.Glue.Probability
import Lib.Layer0.IndepExpect

/-!
# Stochastic Subgradient Method — Convex Non-Smooth Optimization (Primitive Form)

Layer: 2 (concrete algorithm proof with novel structure)

CRITICAL CORRECTION: Removed all abstract subdifferential symbols per Principle A.
Replaced with primitive inequality form: ∀ w s y, f y ≥ f w + ⟪gradL w s, y - w⟫_ℝ
This avoids invented symbols and matches Mathlib's primitive requirements.

Archetype B — novel proof structure bypassing Layer 1 meta-theorems due to:
- Absence of gradF/unbiasedness hypotheses
- Direct use of primitive subgradient inequality in pointwise bound
- Variance bound derived internally from uniform oracle bound (Pattern I)

Proof chain (convex case, constant η):
1. Pointwise norm expansion via `norm_sq_sgd_step`
2. Primitive subgradient inequality: f(wₜ) - f(w*) ≤ ⟪gₜ, wₜ - w*⟫ (direct quantifier form)
3. Substitute inequality into expansion
4. Integrate + linearity
5. Bounded oracle → variance bound via Pattern I
6. Telescope sum + algebraic rearrangement

Design compliance:
- Convention §1: Variance bound derived internally with explicit integrability
- Convention §2: hgL stored in structure (weakest measurability level)
- Convention §4: All lemmas include Used in: tags
- Convention §5: NOT APPLICABLE (uniform oracle bound)

Used in: main convergence result for non-smooth convex stochastic optimization
-/

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped InnerProductSpace

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
  [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]
variable {S : Type*} [MeasurableSpace S]
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]

-- ============================================================================
-- SubgradientSetup: NO gradF field (non-smooth objective)
-- ============================================================================
/-- Complete setup for stochastic subgradient method.
Critical distinction: contains NO `gradF` field (non-smooth objective has no gradient).
Includes oracle measurability `hgL` as structure field (Convention §2). -/
structure SubgradientSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  w₀ : E
  η : ℕ → ℝ
  gradL : E → S → E
  ξ : ℕ → Ω → S
  P : Measure Ω
  hP : IsProbabilityMeasure P
  hξ_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (ξ t)
  hξ_indep : iIndepFun (β := fun _ => S) ξ P
  hξ_ident : ∀ t, IdentDistrib (ξ t) (ξ 0) P P
  hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry gradL)

/-- Self-contained recursive process definition for subgradient method (Archetype B). -/
noncomputable def SubgradientSetup.process (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω) : ℕ → Ω → E
  | 0 => fun _ => setup.w₀
  | t + 1 => fun ω =>
      let w_t := process setup t ω
      w_t - setup.η t • setup.gradL w_t (setup.ξ t ω)

namespace SubgradientSetup

variable (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω)

@[simp]
theorem process_zero : process setup 0 = fun _ => setup.w₀ := by
  rfl

@[simp]
theorem process_succ (t : ℕ) :
    process setup (t + 1) =
      fun ω => process setup t ω - setup.η t • setup.gradL (process setup t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) := by
  rfl

end SubgradientSetup

-- ============================================================================
-- Convergence theorem (PRIMITIVE FORM — NO abstract subdifferential symbols)
-- ============================================================================
/-- Convex convergence for stochastic subgradient method (primitive inequality form).
Archetype B: novel proof structure bypassing Layer 1 meta-theorems.
Uses primitive subgradient condition: ∀ w s y, f y ≥ f w + ⟪gradL w s, y - w⟫_ℝ
Derives variance bound internally from uniform oracle bound (Pattern I).
Used in: main result for non-smooth convex stochastic optimization (this file) -/
theorem subgradient_convergence_convex
    (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω) (f : E → ℝ) {G η : ℝ} (wStar : E)
    (hsubgrad : ∀ w s y, f y ≥ f w + ⟪setup.gradL w s, y - w⟫_ℝ)
    (hbounded : ∀ w s, ‖setup.gradL w s‖ ≤ G)
    (hη_pos : 0 < η) (hη : ∀ t, setup.η t = η)
    (T : ℕ) (hT : 0 < T)
    (h_int_f : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω => f (setup.process t ω)) setup.P)
    (h_int_norm_sq : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.P)
    (h_int_sq : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) setup.P) :
    (1 / (T : ℝ)) * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T,
        (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤
      ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 / (2 * η * T) + η * G ^ 2 / 2 := by
  -- Use the recursive definition of the process
  have h_succ : ∀ t, setup.process (t + 1) = fun ω => setup.process t ω - setup.η t • setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) := by
    intro t
    rw [process_succ]
    rfl

  -- We'll prove the bound by expanding the norm squared of the distance to the optimum
  -- and using the subgradient property to telescope the sum
  have key_bound : ∀ t, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P ≤
                  ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - 
                  2 * η * ∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2 := by
    intro t
    -- Apply norm expansion using norm_sq_sgd_step
    have h_expansion : ∀ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 = 
                       ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 - 
                       2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + 
                       (setup.η t) ^ 2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2 := by
      intro ω
      rw [h_succ, norm_sq_sgd_step]

    -- Integrate both sides
    have h_integrated : ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P =
                        ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - 
                        2 * setup.η t * ∫ ω, ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ ∂setup.P + 
                        ∫ ω, (setup.η t) ^ 2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P := by
      rw [h_expansion]
      -- Apply integral operations
      have h_int1 : Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.P := h_int_norm_sq t
      -- Show integrability of the inner product term
      have h_int2 : Integrable (fun ω => ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ) setup.P := by
        -- From the hypothesis h_int_norm_sq t, we have Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.P
        -- From the hypothesis h_int_sq t, we have Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) setup.P
        -- Using Cauchy-Schwarz inequality: |⟪u,v⟫| ≤ ‖u‖·‖v‖ ≤ (‖u‖^2 + ‖v‖^2)/2
        have h_bound : ∀ ω, |⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ| ≤
                             (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 + ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) / 2 := by
          intro ω
          calc |⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ|
              ≤ ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ := abs_real_inner_le_norm _ _
            _ ≤ (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 + ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) / 2 := by
                -- Prove that for real numbers a, b: a*b <= (a^2 + b^2)/2
                -- This follows from 0 <= (a-b)^2 = a^2 - 2ab + b^2, so 2ab <= a^2 + b^2
                have h_ineq : (2 : ℝ) * ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ≤
                            ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 + ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2 := by
                  have h_sq : 0 ≤ (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ - ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖) ^ 2 :=
                    pow_two_nonneg (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ - ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖)
                  rw [pow_two, sub_mul_self] at h_sq
                  linarith
                field_simp at h_ineq
                linarith
        have h_sum_integrable : Integrable (fun ω => (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 + ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) / 2) setup.P :=
          ((h_int_norm_sq t).add (h_int_sq t)).smul (2⁻¹ : ℝ)
        apply Integrable.mono' h_sum_integrable
        intro ω
        exact h_bound ω
      have h_int3 : Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) setup.P := h_int_sq t
      rw [integral_add h_int1 (integral_smul (-2 * setup.η t) h_int2)]
      rw [integral_smul (setup.η t ^ 2) h_int3]
      ring

    -- Use the subgradient inequality: f y ≥ f w + ⟪gradL w s, y - w⟫
    -- With y = wStar and w = setup.process t ω, we get:
    -- f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫
    have h_subgrad_ineq : ∀ ω, f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫_ℝ := by
      intro ω
      specialize hsubgrad (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) wStar
      linarith

    -- Rearrange to get: ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar
    have h_rearranged : ∀ ω, ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar := by
      intro ω
      have h := h_subgrad_ineq ω
      rw [inner_neg_right, neg_sub] at h
      linarith

    -- Apply this to the integral term
    have h_integral_ineq : ∫ ω, ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ ∂setup.P ≥
                           ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P := by
      -- Need to show integrability of (fun ω => f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar)
      have h_int_diff : Integrable (fun ω => f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar) setup.P :=
        (h_int_f t).sub (integrable_const (f wStar) setup.P)
      apply integral_mono
      . exact h_int2
      . exact h_int_diff
      . intro ω
        rw [inner_comm]
        exact h_rearranged ω

    -- Use the bounded gradient condition to bound the last term
    have h_bounded_term : ∫ ω, (setup.η t) ^ 2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P ≤ η ^ 2 * G ^ 2 := by
      rw [← integral_const_mul, ← hη t]
      apply integral_mono_const_of_ae_le
      intro ω
      rw [sq_le_sq]
      apply pow_mono
      . exact hbounded (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)
      . norm_num

    -- Combine all bounds
    rw [h_integrated]
    linarith [h_integral_ineq, h_bounded_term]

  -- Now we can telescope the sum
  have h_telescope : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤
                     (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) / (2 * η) + T * η * G ^ 2 / 2 := by
    -- We will sum the key_bound inequalities for t from 0 to T-1
    -- Each key_bound t gives us:
    -- ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P ≤
    -- ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - 
    -- 2 * η * ∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2
    -- Rearranging:
    -- 2 * η * ∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P ≤
    -- ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2
    
    -- Summing this for t from 0 to T-2 (which is range (T-1)):
    have h_sum_inequality : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range (T - 1), (2 * η * ∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P) ≤
                           ∑ t ∈ Finset.range (T - 1), (∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - 
                                                      ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) + (T - 1) * η ^ 2 * G ^ 2 := by
      apply Finset.sum_le_sum
      intro t ht
      have h := key_bound t
      -- From key_bound t: ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P ≤
      --                    ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - 
      --                    2 * η * ∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2
      -- Rearranging: 2 * η * ∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P ≤
      --              ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2
      linarith [ht, hT]
    
    -- The telescoping sum on the right
    have h_telescope : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range (T - 1), (∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - 
                                                    ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) =
                      ∫ ω, ‖setup.process 0 ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (T - 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P := by
      rw [Finset.sum_sub_distrib]
      rw [Finset.sum_range_sub_sum_succ]
      . intro t ht
        apply h_int_norm_sq
      . intro t ht
        apply h_int_norm_sq
    
    -- Now we need to relate the left-hand side of the inequality to our target sum
    -- Left-hand side is: ∑ t in range (T-1), (2 * η * ∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P)
    -- This equals: 2 * η * ∑ t in range (T-1), (∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P)
    -- Change of variables: u = t + 1, so when t ranges from 0 to T-2, u ranges from 1 to T-1
    have h_lhs_change : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range (T - 1), (2 * η * ∫ ω, f (setup.process (t + 1) ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P) =
                        2 * η * ∑ u ∈ Finset.Ico 1 T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process u ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P) := by
      rw [Finset.sum_mul, Finset.sum_image]
      . congr
        ext u
        simp [Finset.mem_Ico, Finset.mem_range]
        constructor
        . intro ⟨t, ht, eq⟩
          use t
          constructor
          . linarith [ht.1, ht.2]
          . rw [eq]
        . intro ⟨t, ht, eq⟩
          use t
          constructor
          . constructor
            . linarith [ht.1, hT]
            . linarith [ht.2]
          . rw [← eq]
      . intro t₁ t₂ ht₁ ht₂ h
        injection h with h
        exact h
    
    -- Combine everything
    calc 2 * η * ∑ u ∈ Finset.Ico 1 T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process u ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P) ≤
         ∫ ω, ‖setup.process 0 ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (T - 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P + (T - 1) * η ^ 2 * G ^ 2 := by
      rw [h_lhs_change, h_telescope]
      exact h_sum_inequality
    
    -- Now add the t=0 term to both sides to get the full sum
    calc 2 * η * (∫ ω, f (setup.process 0 ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P + ∑ u ∈ Finset.Ico 1 T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process u ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P)) ≤
         2 * η * (∫ ω, f (setup.process 0 ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P) + 
         (∫ ω, ‖setup.process 0 ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (T - 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P + (T - 1) * η ^ 2 * G ^ 2) := by
      apply add_le_add
      . rw [mul_add]
        apply le_refl
      . assumption
    
    -- Simplify the left side
    _ = 2 * η * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P) := by
      rw [← Finset.sum_range_succ, Finset.sum_Ico_eq_sum_range_add_one]
      . rfl
      . linarith [hT]
    
    -- Simplify the right side and add an extra term to get the T-th element
    _ ≤ (∫ ω, ‖setup.process 0 ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2) + (T - 1) * η ^ 2 * G ^ 2 := by
      apply add_le_add_right
      -- We need to show: - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (T - 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P ≤ - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2
      -- This is equivalent to: ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P ≤ ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (T - 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P + η ^ 2 * G ^ 2
      -- This follows from key_bound (T-1) after rearranging
      sorry -- need to prove this step
    
    -- Final simplification
    _ = (∫ ω, ‖setup.process 0 ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) + T * η ^ 2 * G ^ 2 := by
      ring
    
    -- Use process_zero
    _ = (∫ ω, ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) + T * η ^ 2 * G ^ 2 := by
      rw [process_zero, Pi.zero_apply]
    
    -- Divide both sides by 2*η
    have h_divided : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar ∂setup.P) ≤
                     (∫ ω, ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) / (2 * η) + T * η * G ^ 2 / 2 := by
      have h_pos : 2 * η > 0 := by linarith [hη_pos]
      field_simp [mul_comm, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm] at this
      linarith [h_pos]
    exact h_divided

  -- Final rearrangement
  have h_final : (1 / (T : ℝ)) * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤
                 (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.P) / (2 * η * T) + η * G ^ 2 / 2 := by
    rw [div_eq_mul_inv, mul_assoc]
    have h_div : (2 * η) * (T : ℝ) ≠ 0 := by
      rw [mul_ne_zero_iff]
      constructor
      . linarith [hη_pos]

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

  [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]
variable {S : Type*} [MeasurableSpace S]
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]

-- ============================================================================
-- SubgradientSetup: NO gradF field (non-smooth objective)
-- ============================================================================
/-- Complete setup for stochastic subgradient method.
Critical distinction: contains NO `gradF` field (non-smooth objective has no gradient).
Includes oracle measurability `hgL` as structure field (Convention §2). -/
structure SubgradientSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  w₀ : E
  η : ℕ → ℝ
  gradL : E → S → E
  ξ : ℕ → Ω → S
  P : Measure Ω
  hP : IsProbabilityMeasure P
  hξ_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (ξ t)
  hξ_indep : iIndepFun (β := fun _ => S) ξ P
  hξ_ident : ∀ t, IdentDistrib (ξ t) (ξ 0) P P
  hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry gradL)

/-- Self-contained recursive process definition for subgradient method (Archetype B). -/
noncomputable def SubgradientSetup.process (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω) : ℕ → Ω → E
  | 0 => fun _ => setup.w₀
  | t + 1 => fun ω =>
      let w_t := process setup t ω
      w_t - setup.η t • setup.gradL w_t (setup.ξ t ω)

namespace SubgradientSetup

variable (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω)

@[simp]
theorem process_zero : process setup 0 = fun _ => setup.w₀ := by
  rfl

@[simp]
theorem process_succ (t : ℕ) :
    process setup (t + 1) =
      fun ω => process setup t ω - setup.η t • setup.gradL (process setup t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) := by
  rfl

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped InnerProductSpace

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
  [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]
variable {S : Type*} [MeasurableSpace S]
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]

-- ============================================================================
-- SubgradientSetup: NO gradF field (non-smooth objective)
-- ============================================================================
/-- Complete setup for stochastic subgradient method.
Critical distinction: contains NO `gradF` field (non-smooth objective has no gradient).
Includes oracle measurability `hgL` as structure field (Convention §2). -/
structure SubgradientSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  w₀ : E
  η : ℕ → ℝ
  gradL : E → S → E
  ξ : ℕ → Ω → S
  P : Measure Ω
  hP : IsProbabilityMeasure P
  hξ_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (ξ t)
  hξ_indep : iIndepFun (β := fun _ => S) ξ P
  hξ_ident : ∀ t, IdentDistrib (ξ t) (ξ 0) P P
  hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry gradL)

/-- Self-contained recursive process definition for subgradient method (Archetype B). -/
noncomputable def SubgradientSetup.process (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω) : ℕ → Ω → E
  | 0 => fun _ => setup.w₀
  | t + 1 => fun ω =>
      let w_t := process setup t ω
      w_t - setup.η t • setup.gradL w_t (setup.ξ t ω)

namespace SubgradientSetup

variable (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω)

@[simp]
theorem process_zero : process setup 0 = fun _ => setup.w₀ := by
  rfl

@[simp]
theorem process_succ (t : ℕ) :
    process setup (t + 1) =
      fun ω => process setup t ω - setup.η t • setup.gradL (process setup t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) := by
  rfl

end SubgradientSetup

    (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω) (f : E → ℝ) {G η : ℝ} (wStar : E)

    rw [SubgradientSetup.process_succ]

    -- So f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), -(setup.process t ω - wStar)⟫_ℝ

    ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P := by

  -- Apply the gradient norm bound
  have final_sum_bound : η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P ≤ η^2 * T * G^2 := by

    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  w₀ : E
  η : ℕ → ℝ
  gradL : E → S → E
  ξ : ℕ → Ω → S
  P : Measure Ω
  hP : IsProbabilityMeasure P
  hξ_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (ξ t)
  hξ_indep : iIndepFun (β := fun _ => S) ξ P
  hξ_ident : ∀ t, IdentDistrib (ξ t) (ξ 0) P P
  hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry gradL)

/-- Self-contained recursive process definition for subgradient method (Archetype B). -/
noncomputable def SubgradientSetup.process (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω) : ℕ → Ω → E
  | 0 => fun _ => setup.w₀
  | t + 1 => fun ω =>
      let w_t := process setup t ω
      w_t - setup.η t • setup.gradL w_t (setup.ξ t ω)

namespace SubgradientSetup

variable (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω)

@[simp]
theorem process_zero : process setup 0 = fun _ => setup.w₀ := by
  rfl

@[simp]
theorem process_succ (t : ℕ) :
    process setup (t + 1) =
      fun ω => process setup t ω - setup.η t • setup.gradL (process setup t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) := by
  rfl

end SubgradientSetup

-- ============================================================================
-- Convergence theorem (PRIMITIVE FORM — NO abstract subdifferential symbols)
-- ============================================================================
/-- Convex convergence for stochastic subgradient method (primitive inequality form).
Archetype B: novel proof structure bypassing Layer 1 meta-theorems.
Uses primitive subgradient condition: ∀ w s y, f y ≥ f w + ⟪gradL w s, y - w⟫_ℝ
Derives variance bound internally from uniform oracle bound (Pattern I).
Used in: main result for non-smooth convex stochastic optimization (this file) -/
theorem subgradient_convergence_convex
    (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω) (f : E → ℝ) {G η : ℝ} (wStar : E)
    (hsubgrad : ∀ w s y, f y ≥ f w + ⟪setup.gradL w s, y - w⟫_ℝ)
    (hbounded : ∀ w s, ‖setup.gradL w s‖ ≤ G)
    (hη_pos : 0 < η) (hη : ∀ t, setup.η t = η)
    (T : ℕ) (hT : 0 < T)
    (h_int_f : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω => f (setup.process t ω)) setup.P)
    (h_int_norm_sq : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.P)
    (h_int_sq : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) setup.P) :
    (1 / (T : ℝ)) * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T,
        (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤
      ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 / (2 * η * T) + η * G ^ 2 / 2 := by
  -- For each t, expand the squared distance from the next iterate to the optimal point
  have step_expansion : ∀ (t : ℕ) (ω : Ω),
    ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 = 
      ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
    intro t ω
    rw [SubgradientSetup.process_succ]
    simp
    apply norm_sq_sgd_step
  
  -- Use the subgradient inequality to bound the inner product term
  have subgrad_bound : ∀ (t : ℕ) (ω : Ω),
    ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar := by
    intro t ω
    specialize hsubgrad (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) wStar
    -- From hsubgrad: f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫_ℝ
    have h_temp : f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫_ℝ := hsubgrad
    -- Show wStar - setup.process t ω = -(setup.process t ω - wStar)
    have neg_eq : wStar - setup.process t ω = -(setup.process t ω - wStar) := by abel
    rw [neg_eq] at h_temp
    -- So f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), -(setup.process t ω - wStar)⟫_ℝ
    -- Use the property that ⟪x, -y⟫ = -⟪x, y⟫
    rw [inner_neg_right] at h_temp
    -- So f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ
    -- Rearranging: ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar
    have temp_result : ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar := by
      linarith [h_temp]
    -- By symmetry of inner product: ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ = ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ
    rw [inner_comm]
    exact temp_result
  
  -- Combine to get a recursive bound
  have recursive_bound : ∀ (t : ℕ) (ω : Ω),
    2 * setup.η t * (f wStar - f (setup.process t ω)) ≤
      ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
    intro t ω
    -- From step_expansion: ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 = 
    --   ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2
    -- Therefore: -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 = 
    --   -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2
    have step_expansion_neg : -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 =
      -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
      calc
        -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2
        = -(‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2) := by
          congr
          exact (step_expansion t ω)
        _ = -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
          ring
    calc
      2 * setup.η t * (f wStar - f (setup.process t ω))
      ≤ 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ := by
        have temp := subgrad_bound t ω
        rw [sub_eq_add_neg, neg_sub]
        apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left
        · exact temp.symm.le
        · positivity
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
        ring
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + (-‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2) + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
        ring
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + (-‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2) + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
        rw [←step_expansion_neg]
        ring
  
  -- Since η is constant, we can factor it out
  have h_const : ∀ t, setup.η t = η := hη
  
  -- Sum over t from 0 to T-1
  have sum_inequality : 2 * η * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P) ≤
    ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
    calc
      2 * η * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P)
      = ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, 2 * η * (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P) := (Finset.sum_mul _ _).symm
      = ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, 2 * η * (f wStar - f (setup.process t ω)) ∂setup.P := by
        congr
        ext t
        rw [integral_const_sub (h_int_f t)]
        ring
      ≤ ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 + η^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2) ∂setup.P := by
        apply Finset.sum_le_sum
        intro t ht
        have h := recursive_bound t
        rw [h_const t]
        refine integral_mono (by _) (by _) ?_
        · exact (integrable_const_smul _ (integrable_const_sub (h_int_f t))).2
        · exact (integrable_sub (h_int_norm_sq t) (h_int_norm_sq (t + 1))).add (integrable_const_smul _ (h_int_sq t))
        · intro ω
          exact h ω
      = ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P) := by
        congr
        ext t
        rw [integral_sub (h_int_norm_sq t) (h_int_norm_sq (t + 1)), integral_const_smul, integral_const_sub (h_int_f t)]
        ring
      = (∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - (∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) + η^2 * (∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P) := by
        rw [Finset.sum_add_distrib, Finset.sum_sub_distrib]
      = ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
        congr
        · rw [←Finset.sum_add_dot_Ico_zero_one, Finset.Ico_succ_singleton, Finset.sum_singleton]
        · calc
            ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P
            = ∑ i ∈ Finset.Ico (0+1) (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process i ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.range_eq_Ico, Finset.sum_Ico_eq_add_dot_Ico_succ]
              ring
            = ∑ i ∈ Finset.Ico 1 (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process i ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P
            = (∑ i ∈ Finset.Ico 0 (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process i ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process 0 ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.sum_Ico_succ_singleton_sub, SubgradientSetup.process_zero]
              simp
            = (∑ t ∈ Finset.range (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.range_eq_Ico, SubgradientSetup.process_zero]
              simp
            = ((∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) + ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.sum_Ico_succ_singleton]
        ring
  
  -- Bound the gradient norm terms using the bounded assumption
  have grad_norm_bound : ∀ t, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P ≤ G^2 := by
    intro t
    calc
      ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P
      ≤ ∫ ω, G^2 ∂setup.P := by
        refine integral_mono (h_int_sq t) ?_ ?_
        · exact integrable_const
        · intro ω
          calc
            ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2
            = ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2 := by rfl
            ≤ G ^ 2 := by
              rw [sq_abs]
              apply pow_le_pow_left₀ (norm_nonneg _) (hbounded _ _) 2
      _ = G^2 := by rw [integral_const]
  
  -- Apply the gradient norm bound
  have final_sum_bound : η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P ≤ η^2 * T * G^2 := by
    rw [mul_assoc]
    apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left ?_ (mul_nonneg (by positivity) (by positivity))
    apply Finset.sum_le_card_mul_of_le
    intro t ht
    apply grad_norm_bound
  
  -- Combine everything
  calc
    2 * η * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P)
    ≤ ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * T * G^2 := by
      trans (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P)
      · exact sum_inequality
      · apply add_le_add_of_le_of_le rfl.le final_sum_bound
    ≤ ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 + η^2 * T * G^2 := by
      refine add_le_add_of_le_of_le (sub_le_self _ ?_) (le_refl _)
      exact integral_nonneg (by positivity) (fun ω => by positivity)
  
  -- Rearrange to get the desired form
  have rearrange : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤ (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 + η^2 * T * G^2) / (2 * η) := by
    rw [←neg_sub, ←neg_div, neg_le_neg_iff]
    refine div_le_of_le_mul₀ ?_ (mul_pos hη_pos hT)
    rw [mul_comm, ←mul_assoc]
    exact this
  
  -- Divide by T to get the average
  refine div_le_of_le_mul₀ (rearrange.trans ?_) (by positivity)
  rw [div_mul_comm]
  ring

    -- From hsubgrad: f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫_ℝ
    have h_temp : f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫_ℝ := hsubgrad
    -- Show wStar - setup.process t ω = -(setup.process t ω - wStar)

    have temp_result : ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar := by
      linarith [h_temp]
    -- By symmetry of inner product: ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ = ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ
    rw [inner_comm]
    exact temp_result
  
  -- Combine to get a recursive bound
  have recursive_bound : ∀ (t : ℕ) (ω : Ω),
    2 * setup.η t * (f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar) ≤
      ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
    intro t ω
    -- From step_expansion: ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 = 
    --   ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2
    -- Therefore: -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 = 
    --   -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2
    have step_expansion_neg : -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 =
      -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
      calc
        -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2
        = -(‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2) := by
          congr
          exact (step_expansion t ω)
        _ = -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
          ring
    calc
      2 * setup.η t * (f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar)
      ≤ 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ := by
        apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (subgrad_bound t ω) (by positivity)
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
        ring
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + (-‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2) + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
        ring
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + (-‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2) + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by

        rw [Finset.sum_add_distrib, Finset.sum_sub_distrib]

            = (∑ i ∈ Finset.Ico 0 (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process i ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process 0 ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.sum_Ico_succ_singleton_sub, SubgradientSetup.process_zero]

            = (∑ t ∈ Finset.range (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.range_eq_Ico, SubgradientSetup.process_zero]
              simp
            = ((∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) + ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.sum_Ico_succ_singleton]

  -- Bound the gradient norm terms using the bounded assumption
  have grad_norm_bound : ∀ t, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P ≤ G^2 := by
    intro t
    calc
      ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P

        · exact integrable_const

  have final_sum_bound : η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P ≤ η^2 * T * G^2 := by

    ≤ ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * T * G^2 := by
      trans (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P)

      · apply add_le_add_of_le_of_le rfl.le final_sum_bound
    ≤ ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 + η^2 * T * G^2 := by
      refine add_le_add_of_le_of_le (sub_le_self _ ?_) (le_refl _)
      exact integral_nonneg (by positivity) (fun ω => by positivity)
  
  -- Rearrange to get the desired form

    refine div_le_of_le_mul₀ ?_ (mul_pos hη_pos hT)
    rw [mul_comm, ←mul_assoc]
    exact this
  
  -- Divide by T to get the average
  refine div_le_of_le_mul₀ (rearrange.trans ?_) (by positivity)
  rw [div_mul_comm]
  ring
"
}

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

  [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]

-- ============================================================================
/-- Complete setup for stochastic subgradient method.
Critical distinction: contains NO `gradF` field (non-smooth objective has no gradient).
Includes oracle measurability `hgL` as structure field (Convention §2). -/
structure SubgradientSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  w₀ : E
  η : ℕ → ℝ
  gradL : E → S → E
  ξ : ℕ → Ω → S
  P : Measure Ω
  hP : IsProbabilityMeasure P
  hξ_meas : ∀ t, Measurable (ξ t)
  hξ_indep : iIndepFun (β := fun _ => S) ξ P
  hξ_ident : ∀ t, IdentDistrib (ξ t) (ξ 0) P P
  hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry gradL)

/-- Self-contained recursive process definition for subgradient method (Archetype B). -/
noncomputable def SubgradientSetup.process (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω) : ℕ → Ω → E
  | 0 => fun _ => setup.w₀
  | t + 1 => fun ω =>
      let w_t := process setup t ω
      w_t - setup.η t • setup.gradL w_t (setup.ξ t ω)

namespace SubgradientSetup

variable (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω)

@[simp]
theorem process_zero : process setup 0 = fun _ => setup.w₀ := by
  rfl

@[simp]
theorem process_succ (t : ℕ) :
    process setup (t + 1) =
      fun ω => process setup t ω - setup.η t • setup.gradL (process setup t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) := by
  rfl

end SubgradientSetup

-- ============================================================================
-- Convergence theorem (PRIMITIVE FORM — NO abstract subdifferential symbols)
-- ============================================================================
/-- Convex convergence for stochastic subgradient method (primitive inequality form).
Archetype B: novel proof structure bypassing Layer 1 meta-theorems.
Uses primitive subgradient condition: ∀ w s y, f y ≥ f w + ⟪gradL w s, y - w⟫_ℝ
Derives variance bound internally from uniform oracle bound (Pattern I).
Used in: main result for non-smooth convex stochastic optimization (this file) -/
theorem subgradient_convergence_convex
    (setup : SubgradientSetup E S Ω) (f : E → ℝ) {G η : ℝ} (wStar : E)
    (hsubgrad : ∀ w s y, f y ≥ f w + ⟪setup.gradL w s, y - w⟫_ℝ)
    (hbounded : ∀ w s, ‖setup.gradL w s‖ ≤ G)
    (hη_pos : 0 < η) (hη : ∀ t, setup.η t = η)
    (T : ℕ) (hT : 0 < T)
    (h_int_f : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω => f (setup.process t ω)) setup.P)
    (h_int_norm_sq : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.P)
    (h_int_sq : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2) setup.P) :
    (1 / (T : ℝ)) * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T,
        (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤
      ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 / (2 * η * T) + η * G ^ 2 / 2 := by
  -- For each t, expand the squared distance from the next iterate to the optimal point
  have step_expansion : ∀ (t : ℕ) (ω : Ω),
    ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 = 
      ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
    intro t ω
    rw [SubgradientSetup.process_succ]
    simp
    apply norm_sq_sgd_step
  
  -- Use the subgradient inequality to bound the inner product term
  have subgrad_bound : ∀ (t : ℕ) (ω : Ω),
    ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar := by
    intro t ω
    specialize hsubgrad (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω) wStar
    -- From hsubgrad: f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫_ℝ
    have h_temp : f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), wStar - setup.process t ω⟫_ℝ := hsubgrad
    -- Show wStar - setup.process t ω = -(setup.process t ω - wStar)
    have neg_eq : wStar - setup.process t ω = -(setup.process t ω - wStar) := by abel
    rw [neg_eq] at h_temp
    -- So f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) + ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), -(setup.process t ω - wStar)⟫_ℝ
    -- Use the property that ⟪x, -y⟫ = -⟪x, y⟫
    rw [inner_neg_right] at h_temp
    -- So f wStar ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ
    -- Rearranging: ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar
    have temp_result : ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar := by
      linarith [h_temp]
    -- By symmetry of inner product: ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ = ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ
    rw [inner_comm]
    exact temp_result
  
  -- Combine to get a recursive bound
  have recursive_bound : ∀ (t : ℕ) (ω : Ω),
    2 * setup.η t * (f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar) ≤
      ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
    intro t ω
    have h_pos : 0 < setup.η t := by rw [hη t]; exact hη_pos
    -- From step_expansion: ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 = 
    --   ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2
    -- Therefore: -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 = 
    --   -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2
    have step_expansion_neg : -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 =
      -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
      calc
        -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2
        = -(‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2) := by
          congr
          exact (step_expansion t ω)
        _ = -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
          ring
    calc
      2 * setup.η t * (f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar)
      ≤ 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ := by
        apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (subgrad_bound t ω)
        rw [hη t]
        positivity
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
        ring
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + (-‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2) + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
        ring
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + (-‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2) + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
        rw [←step_expansion_neg]
        ring
  
  -- Since η is constant, we can factor it out
  have h_const : ∀ t, setup.η t = η := hη
  
  -- Sum over t from 0 to T-1
  have sum_inequality : 2 * η * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P) ≤
    ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
    calc
      2 * η * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P)
      = ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, 2 * η * (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P) := (Finset.sum_mul _ _).symm
      = ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, 2 * η * (f wStar - f (setup.process t ω)) ∂setup.P := by
        congr
        ext t
        rw [integral_const_sub (h_int_f t)]
        ring
      ≤ ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 + η^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2) ∂setup.P := by
        apply Finset.sum_le_sum
        intro t ht
        have h := recursive_bound t
        rw [h_const t]
        refine integral_mono (by _) (by _) ?_
        · exact (integrable_const_smul _ (integrable_const_sub (h_int_f t))).2
        · exact (integrable_sub (h_int_norm_sq t) (h_int_norm_sq (t + 1))).add (integrable_const_smul _ (h_int_sq t))
        · intro ω
          exact h ω
      = ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P) := by
        congr
        ext t
        rw [integral_sub (h_int_norm_sq t) (h_int_norm_sq (t + 1)), integral_const_smul, integral_const_sub (h_int_f t)]
        ring
      = (∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - (∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) + η^2 * (∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P) := by
        rw [Finset.sum_add_distrib, Finset.sum_sub_distrib]
      = ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
        congr
        · rw [←Finset.sum_add_dot_Ico_zero_one, Finset.Ico_succ_singleton, Finset.sum_singleton]
        · calc
            ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P
            = ∑ i ∈ Finset.Ico (0+1) (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process i ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.range_eq_Ico, Finset.sum_Ico_eq_add_dot_Ico_succ]
              ring
            = ∑ i ∈ Finset.Ico 1 (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process i ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P
            = (∑ i ∈ Finset.Ico 0 (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process i ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process 0 ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.sum_Ico_succ_singleton_sub, SubgradientSetup.process_zero]
              simp
            = (∑ t ∈ Finset.range (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.range_eq_Ico, SubgradientSetup.process_zero]
              simp
            = ((∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) + ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.sum_Ico_succ_singleton]
        ring
  
  -- Bound the gradient norm terms using the bounded assumption
  have grad_norm_bound : ∀ t, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P ≤ G^2 := by
    intro t
    calc
      ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P
      ≤ ∫ ω, G^2 ∂setup.P := by
        refine integral_mono (h_int_sq t) ?_ ?_
        · exact integrable_const
        · intro ω
          calc
            ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2
            = ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2 := by rfl
            ≤ G ^ 2 := by
              rw [sq_abs]
              apply pow_le_pow_left₀ (norm_nonneg _) (hbounded _ _) 2
      _ = G^2 := by rw [integral_const]
  
  -- Apply the gradient norm bound
  have final_sum_bound : η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P ≤ η^2 * T * G^2 := by
    rw [mul_assoc]
    apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left ?_ (mul_nonneg (by positivity) (by positivity))
    apply Finset.sum_le_card_mul_of_le
    intro t ht
    apply grad_norm_bound
  
  -- Combine everything
  calc
    2 * η * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P)
    ≤ ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * T * G^2 := by
      trans (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P)
      · exact sum_inequality
      · apply add_le_add_of_le_of_le rfl.le final_sum_bound
    ≤ ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 + η^2 * T * G^2 := by
      refine add_le_add_of_le_of_le (sub_le_self _ ?_) (le_refl _)
      exact integral_nonneg (by positivity) (fun ω => by positivity)
  
  -- Rearrange to get the desired form
  have rearrange : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤ (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 + η^2 * T * G^2) / (2 * η) := by
    rw [←neg_sub, ←neg_div, neg_le_neg_iff]
    refine div_le_of_le_mul₀ ?_ (mul_pos hη_pos hT)
    rw [mul_comm, ←mul_assoc]
    exact this
  
  -- Divide by T to get the average
  refine div_le_of_le_mul₀ (rearrange.trans ?_) (by positivity)
  rw [div_mul_comm]
  ring
",

Archetype B — novel proof structure bypassing Layer 1 meta-theorems due to:

2. Primitive subgradient inequality: f(wₜ) - f(w*) ≤ ⟪gₜ, wₜ - w*⟫ (direct quantifier form)
3. Substitute inequality into expansion
4. Integrate + linearity
5. Bounded oracle → variance bound via Pattern I
6. Telescope sum + algebraic rearrangement

- Convention §1: Variance bound derived internally with explicit integrability
- Convention §2: hgL stored in structure (weakest measurability level)
- Convention §4: All lemmas include Used in: tags
- Convention §5: NOT APPLICABLE (uniform oracle bound)

Used in: main convergence result for non-smooth convex stochastic optimization

    have temp_result : ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ ≥ f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar := by
      linarith [h_temp]
    -- By symmetry of inner product: ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ = ⟪setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω), setup.process t ω - wStar⟫_ℝ
    rw [inner_comm]
    exact temp_result
  
  -- Combine to get a recursive bound
  have recursive_bound : ∀ (t : ℕ) (ω : Ω),
    2 * setup.η t * (f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar) ≤
      ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
    intro t ω
    have h_pos : 0 < setup.η t := by rw [hη t]; exact hη_pos
    -- From step_expansion: ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 = 
    --   ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2
    -- Therefore: -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 = 
    --   -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2
    have step_expansion_neg : -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 =
      -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
      calc
        -‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2
        = -(‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2) := by
          congr
          exact (step_expansion t ω)
        _ = -‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
          ring
    calc
      2 * setup.η t * (f (setup.process t ω) - f wStar)
      ≤ 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ := by
        apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left (subgrad_bound t ω)
        rw [hη t]
        positivity
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
        ring
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + (-‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + 2 * setup.η t * ⟪setup.process t ω - wStar, setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ - (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2) + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
        ring
      _ = ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 + (-‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2) + (setup.η t)^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 := by
        rw [←step_expansion_neg]
  
  -- Since η is constant, we can factor it out
  have h_const : ∀ t, setup.η t = η := hη
  
  -- Sum over t from 0 to T-1
  have sum_inequality : 2 * η * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P) ≤
    ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
    calc
      2 * η * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P)
      = ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, 2 * η * (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P) := (Finset.sum_mul _ _).symm
      = ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, 2 * η * (f wStar - f (setup.process t ω)) ∂setup.P := by
        congr
        ext t
        rw [integral_const_sub (h_int_f t)]
        ring
      ≤ ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, (‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 - ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 + η^2 * ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2) ∂setup.P := by
        apply Finset.sum_le_sum
        intro t ht
        have h := recursive_bound t
        rw [h_const t]
        refine integral_mono (by _) (by _) ?_
        · exact (integrable_const_smul _ (integrable_const_sub (h_int_f t))).2
        · exact (integrable_sub (h_int_norm_sq t) (h_int_norm_sq (t + 1))).add (integrable_const_smul _ (h_int_sq t))
        · intro ω
          exact h ω
      = ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P) := by
        congr
        ext t
        rw [integral_sub (h_int_norm_sq t) (h_int_norm_sq (t + 1)), integral_const_smul, integral_const_sub (h_int_f t)]
        ring
      = (∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - (∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) + η^2 * (∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P) := by
        rw [Finset.sum_add_distrib, Finset.sum_sub_distrib]
      = ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
        congr
        · rw [←Finset.sum_add_dot_Ico_zero_one, Finset.Ico_succ_singleton, Finset.sum_singleton]
        · calc
            ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process (t+1) ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P
            = ∑ i ∈ Finset.Ico (0+1) (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process i ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.range_eq_Ico, Finset.sum_Ico_eq_add_dot_Ico_succ]
              ring
            = ∑ i ∈ Finset.Ico 1 (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process i ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P
            = (∑ i ∈ Finset.Ico 0 (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process i ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process 0 ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.sum_Ico_succ_singleton_sub, SubgradientSetup.process_zero]
              simp
            = (∑ t ∈ Finset.range (T+1), ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.range_eq_Ico, SubgradientSetup.process_zero]
              simp
            = ((∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.process t ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) + ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P) - ∫ ω, ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P := by
              rw [Finset.sum_Ico_succ_singleton]
        ring
  
  -- Bound the gradient norm terms using the bounded assumption
  have grad_norm_bound : ∀ t, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P ≤ G^2 := by
    intro t
    calc
      ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P
      ≤ ∫ ω, G^2 ∂setup.P := by
        refine integral_mono (h_int_sq t) ?_ ?_
        · exact integrable_const
        · intro ω
          calc
            ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2
            = ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2 := by rfl
            ≤ G ^ 2 := by
              rw [sq_abs]
              apply pow_le_pow_left₀ (norm_nonneg _) (hbounded _ _) 2
      _ = G^2 := by rw [integral_const]
  
  -- Apply the gradient norm bound
  have final_sum_bound : η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P ≤ η^2 * T * G^2 := by
    rw [mul_assoc]
    apply mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left ?_ (mul_nonneg (by positivity) (by positivity))
    apply Finset.sum_le_card_mul_of_le
    intro t ht
    apply grad_norm_bound
  
  -- Combine everything
  calc
    2 * η * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (f wStar - ∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P)
    ≤ ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * T * G^2 := by
      trans (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 - ∫ ω, ‖setup.process T ω - wStar‖^2 ∂setup.P + η^2 * ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, ∫ ω, ‖setup.gradL (setup.process t ω) (setup.ξ t ω)‖^2 ∂setup.P)
      · exact sum_inequality
      · apply add_le_add_of_le_of_le rfl.le final_sum_bound
    ≤ ‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 + η^2 * T * G^2 := by
      refine add_le_add_of_le_of_le (sub_le_self _ ?_) (le_refl _)
      exact integral_nonneg (by positivity) (fun ω => by positivity)
  
  -- Rearrange to get the desired form
  have rearrange : ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, (∫ ω, f (setup.process t ω) ∂setup.P - f wStar) ≤ (‖setup.w₀ - wStar‖^2 + η^2 * T * G^2) / (2 * η) := by
    rw [←neg_sub, ←neg_div, neg_le_neg_iff]
    refine div_le_of_le_mul₀ ?_ (mul_pos hη_pos hT)
    rw [mul_comm, ←mul_assoc]
    exact this
  
  -- Divide by T to get the average
  refine div_le_of_le_mul₀ (rearrange.trans ?_) (by positivity)
  rw [div_mul_comm]
  ring
"
}
